frameworks.name: reinforcement learning (RPE hypothesis)
frameworks.one_line: phasic dopamine encodes reward prediction errors in basal ganglia

frameworks.name: temporal uncertainty / state estimation
frameworks.one_line: imperfect time-to-reward estimate is added as a key constraint on the learner

core_question: Can RPE models be reconciled with the observed pre-reward dopamine ramps under realistic temporal uncertainty?
key_assumptions: phasic dopamine maps onto RPE signal
learner is unbiased given its state estimate
manipulations: presence vs absence of sensory feedback during the delay
key_findings: with sensory feedback, an unbiased learner produces ramping RPEs before reward
without sensory feedback, post-learning RPEs are flat
conventional fixed-delay RPE models predict convergence to baseline, conflicting with observed ramps
interpretations: dopamine ramps are consistent with the RPE hypothesis once state/temporal uncertainty is included
feedback-driven uncertainty reduction reconciles ramping and flat dopamine observations
